sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented with an ankle injury sustained while playing basketball with his son.
He reported landing awkwardly and twisting his ankle, resulting in bruising and swelling on the outside of the ankle, which is tender to palpation.
He has been experiencing difficulty walking and instability in the ankle, which is a concern for him as he is returning to his workout routine after a period of inactivity.
He also mentioned that his son, who is 10 years old, is a fan of basketball and enjoys playing with his friends, which is a source of joy for the patient.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's ankle is swollen on the lateral side, with exquisite tenderness to palpation over the lateral malleolus.
There is also tenderness over the medial deltoid region.
The skin is intact, with brisk capillary refill and no ecchymosis.
The ankle does not appear to be malaligned.
Test Results: The patient had an X-ray of the right ankle, which showed a displaced lateral malleolar fracture at the Weber C level, with no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a displaced lateral malleolar fracture, which is unstable and requires surgical intervention.
Problem list: Lateral malleolar fracture; unstable ankle; ankle pain; difficulty walking.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam for pain management and swelling reduction.
I recommended that the patient ice the ankle and keep it elevated, especially at work, as he works from home.
I also provided crutches to assist with mobility.
I informed the patient that he may need to sit out for approximately three months, but encouraged him to continue coaching his son's basketball team, as he is cleared to do so.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in approximately three months to assess his progress and plan for rehabilitation and return to exercise.
